name: Benchmark Contamination Diagnostic Dataset (BCDD)
description: 1,388 perturbation-holdout pairs across 7 LLM benchmarks (MMLU, MMLU-PRO, ARC-Challenge, GSM8K, TruthfulQA, HumanEval, MBPP). Each pair contains an original benchmark item paired with a semantically-equivalent perturbation (paraphrase for multiple-choice items, number substitution for grade-school math, identifier rename for code). Designed as a held-out companion set for benchmarks identified as discriminatively saturated by the discriminative half-life framework. Supports diagnosis of surface-form sensitivity, not direct training-data contamination.
citeAs: Anonymous Authors. The Half-Life of LLM Benchmarks: Discriminative Decay and Early Memorization Signals. NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations & Datasets Track (under review).
rai:dataCollection: Original benchmark items were sampled from the public HuggingFace releases of MMLU (cais/mmlu, 228 items), ARC-Challenge (allenai/ai2_arc, 200 random items), GSM8K (openai/gsm8k, 200 random items), MMLU-PRO (TIGER-Lab/MMLU-Pro, 196 items), TruthfulQA (truthful_qa, 200 items), HumanEval (openai_humaneval, 164 items), and MBPP (mbpp/sanitized, 200 items). Each original item was paired with a perturbed counterpart generated by a frontier LLM under a fixed system prompt: GPT-4o-mini at temperature 0.3 generated MMLU/ARC paraphrases (preserving answer-key meaning) and GSM8K number substitutions (preserving operation count and step structure); Claude haiku-4-5 generated MATH structural rewrites and MBPP retro-holdout identifier renames. Total generation cost: approximately $2 in API charges. Per-pair fidelity (original vs. perturbed agreement across four GPT generations) was computed and stored as a per-item field.
rai:dataPreprocessing: Each original-perturbation pair carries: source-benchmark identifier, original item text and gold answer, perturbed item text and gold answer, perturbation type (number_substitution / paraphrase / identifier_rename), per-pair fidelity label (verified/unverified, threshold r >= 0.7 across four GPT generations), and 7-model solver traces. For GSM8K, an equivalence-verified subset (n=125) and a strict-equivalence subset (n=46) are flagged via boolean fields on the same items rather than as separate pairs, so the 1,388 total reflects unique items. The 198-item MMLU-PRO Claude replication set and the 300-item GSM8K item-specificity expansion ship as separate verification artifacts and are NOT counted in the 1,388 total.
rai:dataAnnotation: Item-level fidelity labels were derived from the per-item Pearson correlation between original and perturbed accuracy across four GPT generations (gpt-3.5-turbo, gpt-4-turbo, gpt-4o, gpt-4.1). A 30-item GSM8K rubric spot-check using one author-coder and one frontier LLM under a pre-registered three-criterion rubric (operation type, step count, arithmetic complexity) achieved 96.7% raw agreement (29/30); this is reported as a rubric consistency check, NOT as a multi-rater inter-annotator reliability study. An external 50-item paid IAA on the verified subset will be appended as a v1.1 update.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: Hosted at an anonymized, reviewer-accessible URL at submission time, with the anonymized record remaining accessible to reviewers throughout the review period. Final HuggingFace hosting and a permanent DOI are assigned upon de-anonymization. Versioning follows semantic versioning: minor versions (v1.x) ship CHANGELOG.md with per-pair diffs so downstream studies can pin a specific snapshot. Planned v1.1 update: external 50-item human IAA on the GSM8K verified subset and a doubled (~250-item) verified subset.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. All items are public benchmark questions on academic, mathematical, scientific, code, and general-knowledge content. The original benchmarks have already passed their respective publication-stage IRB/license reviews. No personally identifiable information, sensitive personal data, or human-subject data is present in BCDD.
rai:dataBiases: Three known biases affect the released signals. (1) Subject-content bias on MMLU-PRO: the orig-pert gap is concentrated in language-rich subjects (history +15.5pp, economics +13.1pp, psychology +8.3pp) versus near-zero on STEM subjects. Two readings are observationally entangled in the data: paraphrase-style perturbation is more sensitive on language-rich items, and humanities content may have higher textual-corpus prevalence; users analyzing humanities subsets should treat the gap as an early-warning signal that combines exposure and surface-form sensitivity, not a benchmark-wide contamination claim. (2) Generator-evaluator vendor overlap: GPT-4o-mini and Claude haiku-4-5 (BCDD perturbation generators) share vendors with some of the seven frontier evaluator models. A cross-vendor permutation test on the GSM8K gap distribution gives p=0.94, and Llama-3.3-70B (Together AI, no vendor overlap) shows a +9.5pp gap consistent with GPT (+13.4pp) and Claude (+13.1pp), supporting vendor-invariance. Users extending BCDD beyond the seven source benchmarks should consider a third-vendor generator. (3) Leaderboard-ecosystem bias: discriminative half-life status labels reflect the Open LLM Leaderboard top stratum, which is dominated by open-weights fine-tunes; labels may differ on HELM, Chatbot Arena slices, or vendor-internal leaderboards.
rai:dataLimitations: (a) MATH Lvl 5 boundary case: a verified n=116 subset at fidelity r=0.42 produces a mean gap of -2.0pp because number substitution does not preserve item difficulty on competition mathematics. The MATH subset is shipped only as evidence for the boundary diagnostic and should NOT be treated as a contamination probe. (b) GSM8K perturbation fidelity is low (r=0.27 on the full 200-item set), addressed by the 125-item equivalence-verified subset (gap +8.6pp [+4.3, +13.3]) and 46-item strict subset (+7.1pp [+1.2, +14.6]); the strict subset is small. (c) MMLU-PRO effect size (+2.8pp [+1.1, +4.6]) is modest with bootstrap CI overlapping the null upper edge. (d) Item-level annotation uses a one-author-coder + frontier-LLM rubric consistency check; multi-rater human IAA is deferred to v1.1. (e) The dataset supports diagnosis of benchmark-specific surface-form sensitivity, NOT direct training-data contamination.
rai:dataUseCases: Intended use: (i) stress-testing leaderboard saturation status by comparing original vs. perturbed item-level performance across frontier models; (ii) as a held-out companion set for benchmarks the discriminative half-life flags as degraded (e.g., GSM8K, ARC); (iii) as training material for surface-form-robust evaluation pipelines. NOT intended for: claiming direct training-data contamination of a specific model; ranking models on competition mathematics; replacing source benchmarks (BCDD is a companion set, not a replacement).
rai:dataReleaseLicenses: BCDD perturbation pairs are released under CC BY 4.0, the most permissive license compatible with all source-benchmark licenses. Source-benchmark licenses: GSM8K (MIT), MMLU and MMLU-PRO (MIT, derived from open sources), ARC-Challenge (CC BY-SA 4.0), TruthfulQA (Apache 2.0), HumanEval (MIT), MBPP (CC BY 4.0). Original-item text is included verbatim under each source license; perturbed counterparts are derivative works owned by the authors and released under CC BY 4.0. The OpenAI and Anthropic API Terms of Service that apply to the perturbation generators (GPT-4o-mini, Claude haiku-4-5) permit the use of generated outputs for research and dataset release, provided the source model is acknowledged. No model weights are redistributed.

distribution.name: github-repository
distribution.description: Anonymized GitHub repository hosting BCDD v1.0 and the halflife toolkit at submission time.

distribution.name: bcdd-json-files
distribution.description: One JSON file per source benchmark, each containing the perturbation-holdout pairs for that benchmark with original and perturbed text, gold answers, perturbation type, fidelity label, and per-model solver traces.

recordSet.name: perturbation-pairs
recordSet.description: One record per perturbation-holdout pair (1,388 records total). Field names follow the per-benchmark JSON schemas in the FileSet; common fields are listed below.

field.name: source_benchmark
field.description: Source benchmark identifier (mmlu, mmlu_pro, arc, gsm8k, truthfulqa, humaneval, mbpp). Derived from the JSON filename.

field.name: orig_question
field.description: Original benchmark item text (verbatim from source benchmark, under source license).

field.name: orig_answer
field.description: Gold answer to the original item (chain-of-thought for math, choice key for MCQ, expected output for code).

field.name: pert_question
field.description: Perturbed counterpart text (derivative work, CC BY 4.0).

field.name: pert_answer
field.description: Gold answer to the perturbed item.
